## Source: amazon-inspector (cffe41c34a6702c2b84f2c907dbf451269481608a72724c4b91ebf5d6b4838a6)
This npm package installs itself as a persistent privileged service (Windows SCM service as SYSTEM when installed from an admin shell; systemd/launchd equivalents on other platforms) via scripts/post-install.js, which calls node-windows Service.install() and then sc.exe to set automatic start and crash-restart. Once running, the daemon connects to a publisher-controlled RongCloud IM tenant (appKey bmdehs6pbyyks, DM_SERVER_URL=https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im) and accepts message types that drive local execution: COMMAND messages invoke shell scripts under command/win and command/linux and run `openclaw doctor --fix`; DEVICE_CONTROL messages can disable/enable/delete the install (the delete path uninstalls the service and wipes ~/.claw-bridge/config.json); CHAT_MESSAGE messages are forwarded to a locally-spawned `opencode` LLM agent whose SYSTEM_PROMPT grants it authority to run shell operations such as `pkill -f "openclaw gateway"` and `nohup openclaw gateway`. The worker additionally executes `npm install -g opencode-ai@latest` at startup if opencode is not on PATH, running the resulting third-party LLM agent under the same SYSTEM/root privilege and binding it to localhost:4096. service/updater.js schedules `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` every 6 hours under the service account, giving the publisher a permanent silent-update channel at SYSTEM/root. service/modules/dashboard-collector.js and heartbeat-dashboard.js periodically upload MAC address, host/OS info, and the contents of ~/.openclaw/{agents,projects,tasks} (including AI session metadata — models, providers, token counts, cost) to the same publisher backend every 20-30 seconds. The combined shape — privileged persistent service + remote command channel to an author-controlled IM tenant + LLM-driven shell execution + unpinned privileged auto-update + host/usage telemetry — constitutes a remote-administration backdoor regardless of documentation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
